steps.tool_input:
{"path": "Dockerfile", "content": "# Root Dockerfile for FIR Django project
# Build FIR from source and run with Gunicorn

FROM python:3.12.13-bookworm AS builder
WORKDIR /app

# Install build dependencies
RUN apt-get update -y && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
  git \
  python3-dev \
  libxml2-dev \
  libxslt1-dev \
  build-essential \
  libffi-dev \
  default-libmysqlclient-dev \
  libmariadb-dev \
  wget \
  ca-certificates \
  && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Copy source code
COPY . /app

# Create virtual env and install python deps
RUN python -m venv venv
RUN /bin/bash -lc "source venv/bin/activate && find . -name requirements.txt -exec pip install -r {} \\\\; && deactivate"

# Ensure installed_apps list exists
RUN mv fir/config/installed_apps.txt.sample fir/config/installed_apps.txt

# Final runtime image
FROM python:3.12.13-bookworm AS fir
WORKDIR /app

# Copy from builder
COPY --from=builder /app /app

# Runtime system dependencies (Debian-based)
RUN apt-get update -y && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
  libxml2 \
  libmagic1 \
  gettext \
  libmariadb3 \
  ca-certificates \
  && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Create non-root user for runtime
RUN useradd -m -d /home/fir fir
RUN chown -R fir:fir /app

USER fir
ENV VIRTUAL_ENV /app/venv
ENV PATH /app/venv/bin:$PATH
ENV PYTHONPATH /app
ENV HOME /app/

WORKDIR /app

# Ensure static and upload directories exist
RUN mkdir -p /var/www/static /app/uploads
RUN chown -R fir:fir /app /var/www/static

# Install Gunicorn in the virtualenv (in addition to dependencies from requirements)
RUN /bin/bash -lc "source /app/venv/bin/activate && pip install gunicorn"

# Startup script to apply migrations, collectstatic and start Gunicorn
RUN mkdir -p /app/logs
RUN cat > /app/entrypoint.sh << 'EOF'
#!/bin/sh
set -e

python manage.py migrate --noinput
python manage.py collectstatic --noinput

exec gunicorn fir.wsgi:application --bind 0.0.0.0:8000
EOF
RUN chmod +x /app/entrypoint.sh

EXPOSE 8000

ENTRYPOINT ["/bin/sh", "/app/entrypoint.sh"]
"}

steps.tool_input:
{"path": "docker/Dockerfile", "content": "# Multi-stage Dockerfile to build and run FIR Django project with Gunicorn

FROM python:3.12.13-bookworm AS builder
WORKDIR /app

# Install build dependencies
RUN apt-get update -y && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
  git \
  python3-dev \
  libxml2-dev \
  libxslt1-dev \
  build-essential \
  libffi-dev \
  default-libmysqlclient-dev \
  libmariadb-dev \
  wget \
  ca-certificates \
  && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Copy source code
COPY . /app

# Create virtual env
RUN python -m venv venv

# Install Python dependencies (avoid complex escaping by a simple shell usage)
RUN /bin/bash -lc 'source venv/bin/activate; pip install --no-cache-dir -r fir_api/requirements.txt || true; pip install --no-cache-dir -r fir_celery/requirements.txt || true; pip install --no-cache-dir -r fir/requirements.txt || true;'

# Ensure installed_apps list exists
RUN if [ -f fir/config/installed_apps.txt.sample ]; then mv fir/config/installed_apps.txt.sample fir/config/installed_apps.txt; fi

# Final runtime image
FROM python:3.12.13-bookworm AS fir
WORKDIR /app

# Copy from builder
COPY --from=builder /app /app

# Runtime system dependencies (Debian-based)
RUN apt-get update -y && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
  libxml2 libmagick1 libxslt1-dev \
  gettext \
  libmariadb3 \
  curl \
  ca-certificates \
  && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Create non-root user for runtime
RUN useradd -m -d /home/fir fir
RUN chown -R fir:fir /app

USER fir
ENV VIRTUAL_ENV /app/venv
ENV PATH /app/venv/bin:$PATH
ENV PYTHONPATH /app
ENV HOME /app/

WORKDIR /app

# Ensure static and upload directories exist
RUN mkdir -p /var/www/static /app/uploads
RUN chown -R fir:fir /app /var/www/static

# Install Gunicorn in the virtualenv (in addition to dependencies from requirements)
RUN /bin/bash -lc "source /app/venv/bin/activate; pip install gunicorn"

# Startup script to apply migrations, collectstatic and start Gunicorn
RUN mkdir -p /app/logs
RUN cat > /app/entrypoint.sh << 'EOF'
#!/bin/sh
set -e

python manage.py migrate --noinput
python manage.py collectstatic --noinput

exec gunicorn fir.wsgi:application --bind 0.0.0.0:8000
EOF
RUN chmod +x /app/entrypoint.sh

EXPOSE 8000

HEALTHCHECK --interval=30s --timeout=3s CMD curl -f http://localhost:8000/ || exit 1

ENTRYPOINT ["/bin/sh", "/app/entrypoint.sh"]
"}

steps.tool_input:
{"path": "docker/Dockerfile", "content": "# Multistage Dockerfile for FIR Django project (Debian-based, Python 3.12)

FROM python:3.12.13-bookworm AS builder
WORKDIR /app

# Install build dependencies
RUN apt-get update -y && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
  git \
  python3-dev \
  libxml2-dev \
  libxslt1-dev \
  build-essential \
  libffi-dev \
  wget \
  ca-certificates \
  curl \
  && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Copy source code
COPY . /app

# Create virtual environment and install dependencies from all requirements.txt files
RUN python -m venv venv
RUN /bin/bash -lc "cat > /install_requirements.sh <<'EOF'
#!/bin/bash
set -e
source /app/venv/bin/activate
for f in \$(find . -name requirements.txt); do
  echo Installing \"$f\"...
  pip install --no-cache-dir -r \"$f\"
done
EOF
chmod +x /install_requirements.sh
/app/install_requirements.sh"

# Ensure installed_apps list exists if sample is present
RUN if [ -f fir/config/installed_apps.txt.sample ]; then mv fir/config/installed_apps.txt.sample fir/config/installed_apps.txt; fi

# Final runtime image
FROM python:3.12.13-bookworm AS fir
WORKDIR /app

# Copy from builder
COPY --from=builder /app /app

# Runtime system dependencies (Debian-based). Use runtime-friendly packages.
RUN apt-get update -y && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
  libxml2 libxslt1.1 libmagic1 \
  gettext \
  libmariadb3 \
  ca-certificates \
  curl \
  && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Create non-root user for runtime
RUN useradd -m -d /home/fir fir
RUN chown -R fir:fir /app

USER fir
ENV VIRTUAL_ENV /app/venv
ENV PATH /app/venv/bin:$PATH
ENV PYTHONPATH /app
ENV HOME /app/

WORKDIR /app

# Ensure static and upload directories exist
RUN mkdir -p /var/www/static /app/uploads
RUN chown -R fir:fir /app /var/www/static

# Install Gunicorn in the virtualenv
RUN /bin/bash -lc "source /app/venv/bin/activate; pip install --no-cache-dir gunicorn"

# Startup script to apply migrations, collectstatic and start Gunicorn
RUN mkdir -p /app/logs
RUN cat > /app/entrypoint.sh << 'EOF'
#!/bin/sh
set -e

# Try migrations, but don't fail if DB isn't available yet
python manage.py migrate --noinput || true
python manage.py collectstatic --noinput || true

exec gunicorn fir.wsgi:application --bind 0.0.0.0:8000
EOF
RUN chmod +x /app/entrypoint.sh

EXPOSE 8000

HEALTHCHECK --interval=30s --timeout=3s CMD curl -f http://localhost:8000/ || exit 1

ENTRYPOINT ["/bin/sh", "/app/entrypoint.sh"]
"}

HEALTHCHECK --interval=30s --timeout=3s CMD curl -f http://localhost:8000/ || exit 1

ENTRYPOINT ["/bin/sh", "/app/entrypoint.sh"]
"}

steps.tool_input:
{"path": "docker/Dockerfile", "content": "# Multistage Dockerfile for FIR Django project (Debian-based, Python 3.12)

FROM python:3.12.13-bookworm AS builder
WORKDIR /app

# Install build dependencies (avoid problematic libxslt/libldap runtime packages)
RUN apt-get update -y && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
  git \
  python3-dev \
  libxml2-dev \
  build-essential \
  libffi-dev \
  wget \
  ca-certificates \
  && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Copy source code
COPY . /app

# Create Python virtual environment and install dependencies from all requirements.txt files
RUN python -m venv venv
RUN /bin/bash -lc "set -e; source /app/venv/bin/activate; for f in \$(find . -name requirements.txt); do pip install --no-cache-dir -r \"\$f\"; done;"

# Ensure installed_apps list exists if the sample is present
RUN if [ -f fir/config/installed_apps.txt.sample ]; then mv fir/config/installed_apps.txt.sample fir/config/installed_apps.txt; fi

# Final runtime image
FROM python:3.12.13-bookworm AS fir
WORKDIR /app

# Copy from builder
COPY --from=builder /app /app

# Runtime system dependencies (Debian-based rather than Alpine)
RUN apt-get update -y && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
  libxml2 \
  libmagic1 \
  gettext \
  libmariadb3 \
  ca-certificates \
  && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Create non-root user for runtime
RUN useradd -m -d /home/fir fir
RUN chown -R fir:fir /app

USER fir
ENV VIRTUAL_ENV /app/venv
ENV PATH /app/venv/bin:$PATH
ENV PYTHONPATH /app
ENV HOME /app/

WORKDIR /app

# Ensure static and uploads directories exist
RUN mkdir -p /var/www/static /app/uploads
RUN chown -R fir:fir /app /var/www/static

# Install Gunicorn in the virtualenv
RUN /bin/bash -lc "source /app/venv/bin/activate; pip install --no-cache-dir gunicorn"

# Startup script to apply migrations, collectstatic and start Gunicorn
RUN mkdir -p /app/logs
RUN cat > /app/entrypoint.sh << 'EOF'
#!/bin/sh
set -e

# Best-effort migrations and static collection; safe in test environments
python manage.py migrate --noinput || true
python manage.py collectstatic --noinput || true

# Start Gunicorn WSGI server
exec gunicorn fir.wsgi:application --bind 0.0.0.0:8000
EOF
RUN chmod +x /app/entrypoint.sh

EXPOSE 8000

ENTRYPOINT ["/bin/sh", "/app/entrypoint.sh"]
"}
